Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - {{Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Urashima Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Urashima Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Urashima Shrine—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents * Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties * Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:New]]

Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Urashima Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長|12=draft}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日|12=draft}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日|12=draft}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎|12=draft}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=draft}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|12=draft}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|12=draft}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine -
