{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長|12=draft|12=draft}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日|12=draft|12=draft}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎|12=draft|12=draft}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|12=draft|12=draft}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

revision.comment: 30 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:浦嶋神社 up to rev 98173411
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |名称= 浦嶋神社 |所在地= [[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜191 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 43 | 緯度秒 = 44.0 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 28.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto |祭神= 浦嶋子（[[浦島太郎]]） |社格= 旧[[郷社]] |創建= [[825年]]（[[天長]]2年） |本殿= 本殿神明造 |別名 = 宇良神社 |札所等 = |例祭= 例大祭（[[8月6日]]、[[8月7日]]） |神事= }} '''浦嶋神社'''（うらしまじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[浦島太郎|浦嶋伝説]]が伝わる。'''宇良神社'''（うらじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 [[ファイル:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿]] [[ファイル:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|サムネイル|本殿と中殿]] == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *浦嶋子（[[浦島太郎]]） ;相殿神 *[[月読命]]（つくよみのみこと） *[[祓戸大神]]（はらえどのおおかみ） == 歴史 == 社伝によると、創祀年代は[[平安時代]]、[[淳和天皇]]の[[825年]]（[[天長]]2年）[[7月22日]]とされ、浦嶋子を筒川大明神として祀るのが始めであると伝えられる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=由緒 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=浦嶋神社公式}}。宇良神社とも呼ばれ、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（927年）「[[延喜式神名帳]]」所載によると『宇良神社(うらのかむやしろ)』と記されている。伝承によると、[[淳和天皇]]は、「浦嶋子が、[[雄略天皇]]22年（[[478年]]）7月7日美婦に誘われ常世の国へ行き、347年を経て、[[天長]]2年（[[825年]]）に戻った」との話を聞き、浦嶋子を筒川大明神と名付け、[[小野篁]]を[[勅旨]]として社殿が造営されたとされる。 == 祭事 == * 元旦祭（1月1日） * 節分祭（2月 節分の日） * 宵宮（3月16日日の入り刻） * 祈年祭（3月17日午前9時） * 延年祭（3月17日午前10時） * 例大祭（8月6日-宵宮、7日-例大祭） * 新嘗祭（12月7日） * 月次祭（毎月7日、17日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 紙本著色浦嶋明神縁起 1巻 （絵画）- 1971年（昭和46年）6月22日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=紙本著色浦島明神縁起 ／ 国宝・重要文化財（美術品） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース}}。 :: 室町時代前半頃の[[絵巻物|絵巻]]。絵巻の主題は浦島子伝説だが、宇良神社の縁起として描かれており、物語の後に神社の建立と祭礼の場面がある。説明文は無いが絵の内容として、後世の[[御伽草子]]が一般化する以前の絵入り[[物語]]であり、通常の[[大和絵]]の画風であるが、着色に一種独特の雰囲気があり、以後に盛んとなる御伽草子の先駆的作例で、浦島明神縁起絵として唯一である。 * 刺繍桐桜土筆文肩裾小袖 （工芸品）- 1964年（昭和39年）1月28日{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=刺繍桐桜土筆文肩裾小袖 ／ 国宝・重要文化財（美術品） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 :: 神服、[[安土桃山時代]]。白練緯地の小袖の肩と裾の部分を州浜形に区切って、[[キリ|桐]]、[[サクラ|桜]]、土筆（つくし）[[スミレ|菫]]、[[タンポポ|蒲公英]]などの文様を[[刺繡|刺繍]]で表す。五色の平糸が使用された華麗な構図と手法は桃山時代の特色を表わし、当初の仕立、縫方等を窺うことの出来る資料である。 === 登録有形文化財（国登録） === * 拝殿及び中殿 - 2014年4月15日登録[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 宇良神社拝殿及び中殿（文化遺産オンライン）]。 :: [[1884年]]（明治17年）[[中井権次一統|中井権次]]による建立で、[[1946年]] - [[1965年]]に改修が行われている。木造平屋建、銅板葺、建築面積 93平方メートル。後方に中殿を張出し、広い柱間で桁行三間梁間二間。割拝殿形式で、床上から天井までの約半分程度の壁がなく開放され高欄がつく{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00010002 |title=宇良神社拝殿及び中殿 ／ 登録有形文化財（建造物） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 * 本殿 - 2014年4月15日登録[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 宇良神社本殿（文化遺産オンライン）]。 :: [[1884年]]（明治17年）建立。木造平屋建、茅葺、建築面積26平方メートル{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00010001 |title=宇良神社本殿 ／ 登録有形文化財（建造物） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 === 京都府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本著色浦嶋明神縁起（掛幅本）（絵画） - 1999年（平成11年）3月19日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=京都府指定・登録等文化財その2 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=京都府教育庁指導部文化財保護課}}。 ** 浦嶋社・宇良神社棟札類 10枚（古文書） - 2014年（平成26年）3月24日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=京都府指定・登録等文化財 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=京都府教育庁指導部文化財保護課}}。 ** 紙本墨書続浦嶋子伝記（書跡・典籍） - 2022年（令和4年）3月22日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf 令和4年3月22日京都府公報]}}より京都府教育委員会告示第2号。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 宇良神社祭礼芸能 - 1985年（昭和60年）5月15日指定。 === 京都府登録文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 宇良神社延年祭 - 1988年（昭和63年）4月15日登録。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜191 ; 交通アクセス * [[京都丹後鉄道宮豊線]] [[天橋立駅]] → [[丹後海陸交通|丹海バス]] 浦嶋神社前 → 徒歩 * [[京都縦貫自動車道]] [[宮津天橋立インターチェンジ|宮津天橋立IC]] → [[国道176号]] → [[国道178号]] == 参考文献 == * 伊根町誌編纂委員会 編 『伊根町誌』下巻　1985年、580-609頁。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[丹後国風土記]] *[[祓戸大神]] == 外部リンク == *[https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ 浦嶋神社] - 公式サイト *[http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php 浦嶋神社 - 伊根町観光協会] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うらしましんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:伊根町の建築物]] [[Category:伊根町の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[浦嶋神社]] to [[Urashima Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |名称= 浦嶋神社 |所在地= [[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜191 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 43 | 緯度秒 = 44.0 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 15 | 経度秒 = 28.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto |祭神= 浦嶋子（[[浦島太郎]]） |社格= 旧[[郷社]] |創建= [[825年]]（[[天長]]2年） |本殿= 本殿神明造 |別名 = 宇良神社 |札所等 = |例祭= 例大祭（[[8月6日]]、[[8月7日]]） |神事= }} '''浦嶋神社'''（うらしまじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。[[浦島太郎|浦嶋伝説]]が伝わる。'''宇良神社'''（うらじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 [[ファイル:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿]] [[ファイル:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|サムネイル|本殿と中殿]] == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *浦嶋子（[[浦島太郎]]） ;相殿神 *[[月読命]]（つくよみのみこと） *[[祓戸大神]]（はらえどのおおかみ） == 歴史 == 社伝によると、創祀年代は[[平安時代]]、[[淳和天皇]]の[[825年]]（[[天長]]2年）[[7月22日]]とされ、浦嶋子を筒川大明神として祀るのが始めであると伝えられる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=由緒 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=浦嶋神社公式}}。宇良神社とも呼ばれ、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（927年）「[[延喜式神名帳]]」所載によると『宇良神社(うらのかむやしろ)』と記されている。伝承によると、[[淳和天皇]]は、「浦嶋子が、[[雄略天皇]]22年（[[478年]]）7月7日美婦に誘われ常世の国へ行き、347年を経て、[[天長]]2年（[[825年]]）に戻った」との話を聞き、浦嶋子を筒川大明神と名付け、[[小野篁]]を[[勅旨]]として社殿が造営されたとされる。 == 祭事 == * 元旦祭（1月1日） * 節分祭（2月 節分の日） * 宵宮（3月16日日の入り刻） * 祈年祭（3月17日午前9時） * 延年祭（3月17日午前10時） * 例大祭（8月6日-宵宮、7日-例大祭） * 新嘗祭（12月7日） * 月次祭（毎月7日、17日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 紙本著色浦嶋明神縁起 1巻 （絵画）- 1971年（昭和46年）6月22日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=紙本著色浦島明神縁起 ／ 国宝・重要文化財（美術品） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース}}。 :: 室町時代前半頃の[[絵巻物|絵巻]]。絵巻の主題は浦島子伝説だが、宇良神社の縁起として描かれており、物語の後に神社の建立と祭礼の場面がある。説明文は無いが絵の内容として、後世の[[御伽草子]]が一般化する以前の絵入り[[物語]]であり、通常の[[大和絵]]の画風であるが、着色に一種独特の雰囲気があり、以後に盛んとなる御伽草子の先駆的作例で、浦島明神縁起絵として唯一である。 * 刺繍桐桜土筆文肩裾小袖 （工芸品）- 1964年（昭和39年）1月28日{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=刺繍桐桜土筆文肩裾小袖 ／ 国宝・重要文化財（美術品） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 :: 神服、[[安土桃山時代]]。白練緯地の小袖の肩と裾の部分を州浜形に区切って、[[キリ|桐]]、[[サクラ|桜]]、土筆（つくし）[[スミレ|菫]]、[[タンポポ|蒲公英]]などの文様を[[刺繡|刺繍]]で表す。五色の平糸が使用された華麗な構図と手法は桃山時代の特色を表わし、当初の仕立、縫方等を窺うことの出来る資料である。 === 登録有形文化財（国登録） === * 拝殿及び中殿 - 2014年4月15日登録[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 宇良神社拝殿及び中殿（文化遺産オンライン）]。 :: [[1884年]]（明治17年）[[中井権次一統|中井権次]]による建立で、[[1946年]] - [[1965年]]に改修が行われている。木造平屋建、銅板葺、建築面積 93平方メートル。後方に中殿を張出し、広い柱間で桁行三間梁間二間。割拝殿形式で、床上から天井までの約半分程度の壁がなく開放され高欄がつく{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00010002 |title=宇良神社拝殿及び中殿 ／ 登録有形文化財（建造物） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 * 本殿 - 2014年4月15日登録[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 宇良神社本殿（文化遺産オンライン）]。 :: [[1884年]]（明治17年）建立。木造平屋建、茅葺、建築面積26平方メートル{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00010001 |title=宇良神社本殿 ／ 登録有形文化財（建造物） |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=国指定文化財等データベース ／ 文化庁}}。 === 京都府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 紙本著色浦嶋明神縁起（掛幅本）（絵画） - 1999年（平成11年）3月19日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=京都府指定・登録等文化財その2 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=京都府教育庁指導部文化財保護課}}。 ** 浦嶋社・宇良神社棟札類 10枚（古文書） - 2014年（平成26年）3月24日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=京都府指定・登録等文化財 |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=京都府教育庁指導部文化財保護課}}。 ** 紙本墨書続浦嶋子伝記（書跡・典籍） - 2022年（令和4年）3月22日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf 令和4年3月22日京都府公報]}}より京都府教育委員会告示第2号。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 宇良神社祭礼芸能 - 1985年（昭和60年）5月15日指定。 === 京都府登録文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 宇良神社延年祭 - 1988年（昭和63年）4月15日登録。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[与謝郡]][[伊根町]]本庄浜191 ; 交通アクセス * [[京都丹後鉄道宮豊線]] [[天橋立駅]] → [[丹後海陸交通|丹海バス]] 浦嶋神社前 → 徒歩 * [[京都縦貫自動車道]] [[宮津天橋立インターチェンジ|宮津天橋立IC]] → [[国道176号]] → [[国道178号]] == 参考文献 == * 伊根町誌編纂委員会 編 『伊根町誌』下巻　1985年、580-609頁。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[丹後国風土記]] *[[祓戸大神]] == 外部リンク == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= [[825]] ({{ill|Tenchō (era)|ja|天長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ({{ill|August 6|ja|8月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|August 7|ja|8月7日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{citation needed|Urashima Shrine}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

It is associated with the legend of {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Urashima Shrine]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]]

[[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|ja|淳和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Junna|qq=}} in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Takamura|qq=}} was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]

It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established= [[825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:伊根町の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established= [[825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ine, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:伊根町の建築物]] [[Category:伊根町の歴史]]

It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine]] [[Category:伊根町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Urashima-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine]] [[Category:伊根町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Urashima-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ura-jinja (Ine), torii.jpg|250px]]
Torii |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= }} '''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]] == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō) ; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami) == History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine]] [[Category:伊根町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Urashima-jinja}}
