It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine. == Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|浦嶋神社|version=98173411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Tango Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Urashima Shrine]] [[ja:浦嶋神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ine]] [[Category:伊根の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Urashima-jinja}}

Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)] * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)] === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}} ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}} * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985. === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988. == Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]] == References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|qid=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|qid=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|qq=|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />

It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]

'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)

It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Urashima-jinja]][[ja:浦嶋神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11558239]] Wikidata name: Urashima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Urashima-jinja.JPG |name= Urashima Shrine |location= 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture |latitude_deg = 35 |latitude_min = 43 |latitude_sec = 44.0 |N_or_S = N |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 15 |longitude_sec = 28.1 |E_or_W = E |map_country_code = JP |map = Japan Kyoto |enshrined_deities= Urashimako ([[Urashima Tarō]]) |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:825|825]] ([[:en:Tenchō|Tenchō (era)]] 2) |main_building= Shimmei-zukuri (Main Shrine) |other_names= Ura Shrine |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= festival ([[:en:August 6|August 6]], [[:en:August 7|August 7]]) |ceremonies= |location = {{ill|Ine|WD=Q1345614}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.728889|N|135.257806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Urashima Shrine''' (浦嶋神社, ''Urashima Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture. It is associated with the legend of [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]] and is also known as '''Ura Shrine''' (宇良神社). [[File:Urashima-jinja Worship Hall 001.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:Urashima-jinja Main Sanctuary 001.jpg|thumb|Main Shrine and Middle Shrine]]
== Enshrined Deities == ; Main Enshrined deity * Urashimako (Urashima Tarō)
; Deities in Associated Shrines * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} (Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} (Haraedo-no-Ōkami)
== History == According to shrine tradition, Urashima Shrine was established in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Junna|Emperor Junna]] in [[:en:825|825]] (Tenchō 2, 7th month, 22nd day). It is said that Urashimako was deified as Tsutsukawa Daimyōjin, marking the beginning of the shrine's enshrinement.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/pages/4887675/profile |title=Shrine History |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Urashima Shrine Official Website}}</ref> In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), the shrine is listed as "Ura Shrine" (宇良神社). According to legend, Emperor Junna heard the tale of Urashimako, who was taken to the Land of Eternal Youth (Tokoyo no Kuni) in the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]'s reign (478) and returned 347 years later in 825. Following this, [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] was commissioned to construct the shrine.<ref name=":0" />
== Annual Ceremonies == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February, on Setsubun Day) * Evening Festival (March 16, at dusk) * Kinensai (March 17, 9:00 a.m.) * Ennen Festival (March 17, 10:00 a.m.) * festival (August 6 - Evening Festival, August 7 - Main Festival) * Niinamesai (December 7) * Monthly Festivals (7th and 17th of each month)
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin) (Painting) - Designated on June 22, 1971.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2391 |title=Illustrated Scroll of the Legend of Urashima Myōjin |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref> * Embroidered Kosode with Paulownia, Cherry Blossom, and Horsetail Patterns (Textile) - Designated on January 28, 1964.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6881 |title=Embroidered Kosode |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
=== Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall and Middle Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/286159 Ura Shrine Worship Hall and Middle Shrine (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref> * Main Shrine - Registered on April 15, 2014.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/230928 Ura Shrine Main Sanctuary (Cultural Heritage Online)]</ref>
=== Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Paintings ** ''Urashima Myōjin Engi'' (Hanging Scroll) - Designated on March 19, 1999.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=271 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> * Documents ** Urashima Shrine and Ura Shrine Pillar Plaques (10 items) - Designated on March 24, 2014.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 |title=Kyoto Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-06-30 |publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education}}</ref> ** ''Zoku Urashimako Denki'' (Historical Document) - Designated on March 22, 2022.<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_294_0.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Public Notice, March 22, 2022]}}</ref> * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ritual Performances - Designated on May 15, 1985.<ref name=":1" />
=== Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** Ura Shrine Ennen Festival - Registered on April 15, 1988.<ref name=":1" />
== Access == ; Address * 191 Honjo-hama, Ine-cho, Yosa-gun, Kyoto Prefecture
; Transportation * [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] to [[:en:Amanohashidate Station|Amanohashidate Station]] → [[Tango Kairiku Kotsu]] Bus to Urashima Shrine-mae → Walk * [[:en:Kyoto-Jukan Expressway|Kyoto-Jukan Expressway]] to [[Miyazu-Amanohashidate IC]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Route 176]] → [[:en:Japan National Route 178|Route 178]]
== References == * Ine Town History Compilation Committee, ''Ine Town History'' (Volume 2), 1985, pp. 580–609.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Tango Fudoki|ja|丹後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Fudoki|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368517}} * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://urano-kamuyashiro.amebaownd.com/ Urashima Shrine Official Website] * [http://ine-kankou.jp/kankou/sightseeing/000046.php Urashima Shrine - Ine Town Tourism Association]
